info.title: ADH Backend
info.description: Generated from the Drizzle schema. CRUD routes are derived per table (/<schema>/<resource>); the non-CRUD surface (auth, oauth, chat, registry, customer, storage, search, messaging, notifications, and more) is hand-written in src/openapi/paths/* and kept in sync with the routes by a drift guard.

publicProfileEnabled.description: Whether the public profile card is visible at /public/users/:slug

methods.description: Second factors the user can satisfy

scope.description: REST path prefixes this token may reach (e.g. ["/content/markdown"], optional ":read" suffix, "*" = all). null = legacy curated-only.

token.description: The raw token value — shown exactly once

secret.description: whsec_-prefixed Standard Webhooks signing secret — returned exactly once. Store it; it cannot be retrieved again.

targetKind.description: Namespace of the target entity (e.g. content.markdown)

claimedAt.description: Drizzle-read timestamp of when the job was claimed

leaseExpiresAt.description: Drizzle-read timestamp when the lease expires

leaseToken.description: Opaque lease token required for heartbeat / complete / fail

targetKind.description: Namespace of the target entity (e.g. content.markdown)

status.description: Current lifecycle status (queued | claimed | succeeded | dead)

attempts.description: Number of claim attempts so far

maxAttempts.description: Maximum allowed claim attempts before dead-lettering

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the job was enqueued

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last status transition

prefix.description: Non-secret leading chars of the secret, for display

bucketRdid.description: the token’s own isolated bucket, e.g. storage.<owner-slug>.<name>

prefix.description: Non-secret leading chars of the secret, for display

bucketRdid.description: the token’s own isolated bucket, e.g. storage.<owner-slug>.<name>

token.description: the raw `adh_…` secret — shown once, never again

type.description: URI identifying the problem type; 'about:blank' when unspecified

title.description: Short, human-readable summary of the problem type

status.description: HTTP status code, duplicated in the body

instance.description: URI reference for this occurrence (the request path)

syncSettings.description: Caller-tunable sync settings (gmailLabelIds / redditSubreddits / …) — non-secret, returned so settings forms can prefill instead of blind-overwriting.

configFields.description: For api_key providers: declarative config/credential fields, rendered + validated identically at ecosystem and user scope (absent for OAuth-style providers).

config.description: Non-secret config: clientId, scopes, URLs, endpoints, credentialStyle

hasSecret.description: Whether a client secret is stored (the value is never returned)

config.description: Non-secret config: clientId, scopes, URLs, endpoints, credentialStyle

hasSecret.description: Whether a client secret is stored (the value is never returned)

ecosystemId.description: Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)

state.description: The HMAC-signed state returned by the auth-url endpoint (CSRF)

ecosystemId.description: Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)

fields.description: The provider's declared config fields (configFields), keyed by field key; validated + split into the secret vs non-secret config against the spec.

ecosystemId.description: Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)
Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)
Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)

oneOf.description: actionType=send — an outbound email; at least one body is required
actionType=post — an outbound social post; requires text or media
actionType=submit — a Reddit-style subreddit submission; exactly one of url (link post) or non-empty text (self post) is required (an empty-string text counts as absent)

subreddit.description: Subreddit name without the r/ prefix

url.description: Link post target (mutually exclusive with text)

oneOf.description: actionType=comment — a Reddit reply (reuses the post shape); requires non-empty text and replyTo (a t1_/t3_ fullname); media is rejected by the handler

replyTo.description: The t1_/t3_ fullname being replied to

deduped.description: true when an Idempotency-Key replay returned a prior row

error.description: Failure message when status is error

ecosystem.description: Target ecosystem address (rdid or uuid). Absent = the hub. Unknown/deleted → 404.

EcoManagedRow.description: A row from the ecosystem-scoped invitation surface (pending user, invitation, invitation request, admin note, or history entry). Intentionally open — the shape mirrors the admin routes 1:1, so no fixed property schema is repeated here.

BucketRow.description: A bucket-table row. Intentionally open — the column set is defined at runtime by the developer-managed table this bucket points at, so no fixed property schema applies.

clientMessageId.description: Idempotency key for the user message

ChatStreamEvent.description: One event in the SSE chat stream. The SSE `event:` name carries the discriminator (`open` | `token` | `tool_call_started` | `tool_call_completed` | `done` | `error`); the JSON `data:` payload is one of the shapes below.

oneOf.title: ChatTokenEvent
oneOf.description: token — a chunk of streamed assistant text

oneOf.title: ChatToolCallStartedEvent
oneOf.description: tool_call_started — the model invoked a tool

oneOf.title: ChatErrorEvent
oneOf.description: error — the turn failed; the message explains why (HTTP stays 200)

dmAudienceMask.description: AUDIENCE bitmask of who may DM the user (0 = nobody; default PUBLIC|HUB = 3)

presenceAudienceMask.description: AUDIENCE bitmask of who may see the user presence (0 = nobody; default PUBLIC|HUB = 3)

appearOffline.description: When true, presence is hidden from EVERYONE regardless of the mask

colorMode.description: The user's colour scheme; 'auto' follows the operating system

prefs.description: Empty object when the user has never saved a preference (client defaults apply)

minPoints.description: Inclusive point floor for this rung

subjectType.description: NULL = applies to any subject kind

awarded.description: false when the subject has opted out

refreshToken.description: Opaque customer-session refresh token (single-use; rotated on refresh)

customerId.description: The stable ADH customer id for this externalId

communityId.description: The community instance this topic belongs to.

customerId.description: Author (server-stamped principal). Omitted on the public surface.

excerpt.description: LIST routes only: the opening post’s leading prose, flattened from markdown server-side (≤240 chars; null when its body doc was soft-deleted). Absent on single-topic reads and create/update responses.

authorName.description: Authed COMMUNITY list route only: the author’s display name. Never on the public surface or the flat /discussion/topics list.

authorSlug.description: Authed COMMUNITY list route only: the author’s public handle. Never on the public surface or the flat /discussion/topics list.

authorAvatarUrl.description: Authed COMMUNITY list route only: the author’s avatar URL. Never on the public surface or the flat /discussion/topics list.

bodyDocumentId.description: The content.markdown doc backing this post’s body.

customerId.description: Author (server-stamped principal). Omitted on the public surface.

communityId.description: The community instance (inherited from the topic; present on create only).

content.description: Resolved markdown body (present on the list routes; null if the body doc was deleted).

discussionCount.description: Live discussions filed under this category (public-only for anonymous callers).

lastActivityAt.description: Most recent activity across the category’s live discussions (null when empty).

bio.description: The member’s one-line "what I’m building" intro for this community.

postCount.description: Live posts by this member in this community.

activeCategoryIds.description: Distinct category ids this member has posted in.

StringList.title: StringList
StringList.description: A wrapper object `{ items: string[] }` (NOT a bare array). Returned by the tag/category facet and autocomplete endpoints.

customerId.description: The CREATOR/author (public routes key on this).

ownerKind.description: The kind of principal that OWNS the doc (what access scopes by).

ownerId.description: The owning principal (customer or organization id); server-stamped from the verified ?workspace= scope, else the creator.

visibility.description: Publication state: 'public' is readable on the public surface, 'private' is a draft.

stage.description: Editorial stage: 'draft' is editable; 'final' is immutable (content/title edits + version writes are rejected).

kind.description: Document kind: 'paper' (ordinary) or 'research' (AI-ingested, carries an adh_source frontmatter key).

publicRoute.description: Public route slug (non-null iff visibility='public'); unique per author.

currentVersion.description: The current revision number (incremented on every edit).

ownerKind.description: Inherited from the head document at write.

ownerId.description: Inherited from the head document at write.

title.description: Document title at this revision (full-state snapshot).

sentBy.description: Admin user id who issued the send

title.description: Derived from the doc’s first line.

customerId.description: the customer (user) who created the project

archivedAt.description: ISO timestamp when archived; null = not archived

ownerKind.description: the kind of principal that OWNS the project

ownerId.description: the owning principal (customer or organization id); server-stamped from the verified ?workspace= scope, else the creator

key.description: stable identifier, unique within the project
stable identifier, unique within the project

options.description: the choices for a select field (non-empty); null for every other type

statusId.description: the board column this card sits in

parentId.description: a parent work item in the same project

value.description: the field's stored value for this item (type matches the field's type); null when unset

dependsOnId.description: the work item this one depends on

workItemId.description: set when the event is card-scoped

action.description: the event, e.g. project.created/updated/deleted, status.*, participant.*, work_item.created/updated/status_changed/assigned/deleted, comment.added

direction.description: whether the project INGESTED or PRODUCED this target

deletedAt.description: set when un-linked (soft delete); null for a live link

customerId.description: Owning user; empty for a global memory

failed.description: Rows that failed to embed (retried next call)

more.description: True if the batch filled `limit` — call again

summary.description: Display summary read from the doc frontmatter (jsonb), or null.

evaluation.description: Display evaluation read from the doc frontmatter (jsonb), or null.

snippet.description: ~200-char match-context excerpt around the first q hit (head of content when q is empty/absent).

entityId.description: The UUID the rdid resolves to

toolName.description: curated tool name, or the gateway tool 'adh_request'

decidedBy.description: the human who decided (null until then)

result.description: the outcome once an approved action runs (null until then)

autonomous.description: true = this grant skips the approval gate (except acting as self, which always gates)

source.description: null for curated internal tools; else the source id (e.g. "web", "mcp.<server>")

granted.description: true iff this persona currently holds the tool

autonomous.description: true = this grant skips the approval gate (except acting as self, which always gates); false when not granted

source.description: null for curated internal tools; else the source id (e.g. "web", "mcp.<server>")

allowed.description: true iff the calling user has allowed the persona to invoke this tool for them

kinds.description: the subject kinds this persona may act for (always includes 'self')

lastUsedAt.description: null until the token first authenticates

personaId.description: The persona this token authenticates AS

token.description: The raw token value — shown exactly once, on mint

url.description: The remote MCP server's http(s) endpoint URL

enabled.description: Opt-in: a server contributes no tools until an operator enables it

hasAuthSecret.description: true iff a per-server bearer secret is stored; the secret itself is NEVER returned

createdBy.description: The admin who registered it (null for system rows)

memberKind.description: which principal userId names — 'customer' (customer.customers) or 'persona' (persona.personas). Present on every response: GET list rows, the add-persona 201, and the by-email add 201 (always a customer).

userId.description: the member principal id — a customer id, or a persona id when memberKind is 'persona'

email.description: the member's email (resolved; null for a persona member or a foreign customer)

personaSlug.description: the persona's slug when memberKind is 'persona' (null for a customer member)

itemVerbs.description: top-level item verbs, comma-letter subset of 'C,R,U,D,M' (M = manage access)

defaultFor.description: which member kind holds this role implicitly as the workspace default

restricted.description: present on item-scoped listings: whether the item is RESTRICTED

decidedBy.description: per-verb provenance (the explainer): verb → {kind: 'owner'|'admin-role'|'grant', roleSlug?, via?, scopeItemId?}; sub-item verbs keyed 'sub:<verb>'

objectKey.description: R2 object key the presigned URLs address.

uploadUrl.description: Presigned PUT URL — upload the bytes directly to R2.

used.description: Bytes currently used by ready attachments.

quota.description: The caller’s total quota in bytes.

requested.description: Declared size of the rejected upload.

url.description: Presigned GET URL — download the bytes directly from R2.

quotaBytes.description: Per-user quota in bytes (override or 5 GB default)

usageBytes.description: Live sum of ready, non-deleted attachment bytes

ecosystemId.description: Ecosystem id the override is scoped to

isAdmin.description: Admin of any of the org's teams (the roster's strongest role)

addedAt.description: Earliest membership across the org's teams

post.summary: Sign in (may require a second factor)

identifier.description: Email, user id, or a verified phone number (E.164) — classified server-side

post.summary: Send the SMS login code to the verified primary phone
Complete login by satisfying the second factor → aal=2 session
WebAuthn 2nd-factor: assertion options bound to the pending challenge
WebAuthn 2nd-factor: verify the assertion → aal=2 session
Passwordless passkey: assertion options for an account
Passwordless passkey: verify the assertion → aal=2 session

patch.summary: Update the caller's own profile (name, slug, avatar, public-profile toggle)

schema.description: At least one profile field is required.

get.summary: Check whether a login slug is available to the caller
List the caller’s API tokens (metadata only; raw values never returned)

post.summary: Mint an API token (raw value shown once)

scope.description: REST path prefixes the token may reach

get.summary: List available REST path-prefix scopes that may be assigned to an API token

prefixes.description: Distinct 2-segment path prefixes derived from the live OpenAPI spec (e.g. "/content/markdown")

get.summary: List an application’s API tokens (metadata only; raw values never returned)

post.summary: Mint an API token for the application (raw value shown once)
post.description: The application must belong to the caller’s ecosystem (404 otherwise). Minting also grants the app CRUD on its ecosystem’s default bucket so the token works immediately.

delete.summary: Revoke one of the application’s API tokens

post.summary: Move an ecosystem-scoped row (and its aggregate children) to another ecosystem (admin)

schema.description: Postgres schema of the row’s table

id.description: row id (uuid, or an rdid for rdid-addressed tables)

dryRun.description: report what would move without writing

get.summary: List all capability grants, or one user via ?userId (admin)

post.summary: Grant a capability to a user (admin)
Revoke a capability from a user (admin)
Create an OAuth client + issue its app token (admin)

get.summary: Get a client + its linked providers (admin)

patch.summary: Update a client (admin)

delete.summary: Delete a client (admin; internal clients are protected)

put.summary: Link a provider to a client, optional per-client credential override (admin)

delete.summary: Unlink a provider from a client (admin)

post.summary: Create a provider from a template or full spec (admin)

patch.summary: Update a provider (admin)

get.summary: OAuth providers available for a client

post.summary: Complete an OAuth sign-in (SPA flow) → token + refreshToken + user

get.summary: Begin the server redirect flow → 302 to the provider
Provider redirect target → 302 back with a single-use exchange code

post.summary: Trade a single-use exchange code → token + refreshToken + user

get.summary: Central SSO authorize — silent re-login from the central session, else → central login

302.description: redirect to returnUrl with a fresh exchange code, or to the central login page

post.summary: Central credential login → sets the SSO session, returns the brand redirect URL

200.description: SSO session established; navigate the browser to redirectUrl

get.summary: Central single-logout (browser) — revoke + clear the SSO cookie, then 302 back

302.description: redirect to the validated return (when clientId + return are given)

post.summary: Run an AI action on demand for a saved target
post.description: Loads the saved target server-side and enqueues a high-priority (interactive) job. Idempotent per content version via the dedup key. Returns 202 with the job id.

targetId.description: Row id of the saved entity

jobType.description: Optional; defaults to the registered job for the targetKind

get.summary: Read a single job (poll for completion)
Server-sent completion stream for a job
get.description: Opens an SSE stream that emits one `job` event ({ id, status, result }) when the job reaches a terminal state, then closes. Emits immediately if already terminal. Closes after 60s with no event if still running (client should fall back to polling).

post.description: Atomically claim up to `batch` queued jobs for this worker. Returns an empty array when no jobs are available — never blocks. Ecosystem-scoped via RLS to the caller's api-token.

jobTypes.description: Filter to specific job types; absent = any type

minPriority.description: Claim only jobs with priority >= this value (interactive lane = 100)

workerId.description: Label for the claimed rows; defaults to api-token name or userId

post.summary: Extend the lease on a claimed job
post.description: Workers call this periodically while processing a slow job to prevent the reaper from reclaiming it. Returns 404 when the lease guard fails.
Stores the result jsonb and transitions the job to succeeded. Returns 404 when the lease guard fails (wrong token, wrong ecosystem, or already finished).
Records an error and either re-queues the job with exponential backoff (when retry:true and attempts remain) or moves it to dead. Returns 404 when the lease guard fails.

willRetry.description: True when the job was re-queued

post.description: Ad-hoc / explicit enqueue path (task family 3). Requires a user JWT. The caller's ecosystemId becomes the owning ecosystem. Returns { id, deduped }.

targetKind.description: Namespace of the target entity (e.g. content.markdown)

payload.description: Arbitrary job-specific input stored as jsonb

priority.description: Higher value = claimed first; defaults to 0

id.description: ID of the enqueued (or existing deduped) job

deduped.description: True when an existing job with the same dedupKey was returned

get.description: List the caller's ecosystem jobs, newest first. Requires a user JWT. Optional ?status= filter and ?limit= cap (default 50, max 200).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter to a specific job lifecycle status; absent = all statuses

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return; default 50, capped at 200

post.description: Register a new outbound webhook target. The signing secret (`secret`) is returned exactly once in the response — store it; it cannot be retrieved again. The URL must be https:// and resolve to a publicly routable address (SSRF guard).

url.description: HTTPS delivery target. Private/loopback/link-local URLs are rejected.

get.description: List active (non-deleted) webhook endpoints for the caller's ecosystem. The `secret` field is never included in list responses.

patch.summary: Update a webhook endpoint (active / eventTypes)
patch.description: Toggle `active` or replace `eventTypes`. The URL and secret are immutable after creation. Returns 404 when not found or owned by another ecosystem.

delete.description: Marks the endpoint as deleted (sets `deleted_at`). The row is preserved for audit but hidden from future list results and excluded from deliveries. Returns 404 when not found, already deleted, or owned by another ecosystem.

get.summary: List delivery attempts for a webhook endpoint
get.description: Returns up to 200 delivery-attempt rows for the endpoint, newest first. Returns 404 when the endpoint is not found, soft-deleted, or belongs to another ecosystem.

get.summary: Get an application’s schema permissions (per bucket + per table CRUD)

schemaId.description: the bucket id the grant is for

put.description: Persists the app’s grants as per-app bucket access-groups. Buckets must belong to the app’s ecosystem (404 otherwise); each table must belong to its bucket.

schemaId.description: the bucket id the grant is for

get.summary: List the caller’s token principals (metadata only; secret never returned)

parameters.name: ecosystemId
parameters.description: Narrow to tokens bound to this ecosystem (rdid or uuid; must be manageable by the caller) — the per-product Tokens view

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope every op to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own tokens. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

post.summary: Mint a token principal (raw `adh_…` secret shown once)

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope every op to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own tokens. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

ecosystemId.description: The ecosystem (rdid or uuid; must be manageable by the caller) the token — and its isolated bucket — binds to. Omitted: the owner’s own ecosystem.

delete.summary: Revoke a token principal (also tears down its bucket)

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope every op to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own tokens. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

get.summary: List a target ecosystem's connections (secrets redacted)
get.description: Returns every integration connection OWNED by the target ecosystem `ecosystemId` (the caller must manage it). Secret columns are never included. 400 when `ecosystemId` is omitted; 404/403 when the ecosystem is unknown / not the caller's.

get.summary: Get the OAuth authorize URL for a provider
get.description: Only valid for oauth providers (400 otherwise). 404 for an unknown provider. The authorize URL uses the target ecosystem `ecosystemId`'s client id; the caller must manage it (400 when omitted; 404/403 when unknown / not the caller's).

post.description: Only valid for plaid_link providers (400 otherwise). 404 for an unknown provider. The link token is minted for the target ecosystem `ecosystemId`; the caller must manage it (404/403 when unknown / not the caller's).

ecosystemId.description: Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)

serviceType.description: Defaults to the provider primary service type

post.description: Only valid for oauth_instance providers (400 otherwise). 404 for an unknown provider. The instance is registered under the target ecosystem `ecosystemId`; the caller must manage it (404/403 when unknown / not the caller's).

ecosystemId.description: Target ecosystem id (the caller must manage it)

post.summary: Connect an integration (polymorphic by auth method)
post.description: Finishes any auth method's connect flow and persists the connection under the target ecosystem `ecosystemId` the client names; the caller must manage it (404/403 otherwise). Returns the redacted connection (tokens never echoed).

delete.description: Soft-deletes the connection and tombstones every synced-data row it produced. The owning ecosystem is derived from the connection; the caller must manage it. 404 when the connection is absent/deleted; 403 when the caller cannot manage its ecosystem.

post.description: Runs the connection's worker once, independent of the background scheduler. The owning ecosystem is derived from the connection; the caller must manage it. 404 when absent/deleted; 403 when the caller cannot manage its ecosystem; 503 when no worker is registered for its provider:serviceType (retryable once a worker ships).

Performs one outbound action (e.g. send an email, post a status) as the connection, and records exactly one action-log row. The provider is derived from the connection (never in the URL). 404 when not the caller's; 422 when no action handler is registered for its provider:actionType; 400 on an unknown actionType or invalid body. An optional `Idempotency-Key` header makes a repeat collapse onto the first row (no re-perform).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional client key; a repeat returns the first recorded result.

patch.summary: Update per-connection sync settings
patch.description: Ecosystem-authorized, validated write of the caller-tunable sync settings the sync worker reads (e.g. gmailLabelIds / gmailWindowDays / redditSubreddits / redditKeywords). The owning ecosystem is derived from the connection; the caller must manage it. 404 when absent/deleted; 403 when the caller cannot manage its ecosystem; 400 on an invalid body.

gmailLabelIds.description: Gmail label ids to sync (default INBOX).

gmailWindowDays.description: Days of history to sync (default 30, max 366).

redditSubreddits.description: Subreddits to watch (name or r/name; max 50).

redditKeywords.description: Keywords to match within watched subreddits (max 50).

get.summary: List an ecosystem's provider configs (secrets masked)
get.description: Every stored provider config for the ecosystem, secrets masked to `hasSecret`. The ecosystemId must be the caller's own ecosystem (403 otherwise).

get.summary: Get one of an ecosystem's provider configs (secret masked)
get.description: 404 for an unknown provider or no stored row.

put.description: Replaces the non-secret config; a blank/absent clientSecret preserves the stored encrypted secret, a present one is encrypted at rest. The change invalidates the resolver cache so the ecosystem's new creds drive its flows immediately. The ecosystemId must be the caller's own ecosystem (403 otherwise).

schema.description: OAuth providers send clientId/scopes/…/clientSecret; api_key providers send the spec-driven `fields` map (+ optional `enabled`). The route branches by auth method.

fields.description: api_key providers: the provider's configFields keyed by field key (the one secret is split into the encrypted slot; a blank/absent secret preserves the stored one).

enabled.description: api_key providers: false pauses the provider without deleting its secret.

get.summary: List provider global configs (admin; secrets masked)
get.description: Paged by limit/offset (limit defaults to 50, capped at 100; offset defaults to 0). Returns a pagination envelope: `items` is the page; `total` is the unfiltered provider-config count; `limit`/`offset` echo the applied paging.

get.summary: Get one provider global config (admin; secret masked)

put.description: Merges the non-secret config; a blank/absent clientSecret preserves the stored encrypted secret, a present one is encrypted at rest.

post.summary: Request to join an ecosystem (unauthenticated). Optionally addressed by ecosystem rdid/uuid; defaults to the hub.
Complete an invite-backed account by verifying its email link.

get.summary: Preview an invitation by token (state + masked destination + ecosystem name).

post.summary: A logged-in user accepts an invitation by token.
Add pending users to an ecosystem (owner-scoped)

delete.summary: Delete a pending user from an ecosystem (owner-scoped)
Delete a sent invitation from an ecosystem (owner-scoped)

get.summary: List a subject’s admin notes within an ecosystem (owner-scoped)

put.summary: Reconcile a subject’s admin notes within an ecosystem (owner-scoped)

get.summary: List a subject’s entity history within an ecosystem (owner-scoped)

patch.summary: Enable or disable an auth method setting, upserting it (admin)

get.summary: List the caller's own auth methods

delete.summary: Remove one of the caller's own auth methods (keeping at least one)

post.summary: Set or replace the caller's email/password credential
Link a verified OAuth provider identity to the caller

get.summary: List rows of a bucket-type table (acting as an app/persona)

post.summary: Create a bucket-type row (acting as an app/persona)

put.summary: Update a bucket-type row (acting as an app/persona)

delete.summary: Delete a bucket-type row (acting as an app/persona)

get.summary: List access groups in a bucket

post.summary: Create an access group in a bucket

get.summary: List every access group the caller can see, across all buckets
Get an access group with its members + grants

patch.summary: Rename / edit an access group

schema.description: At least one of name or description is required.

delete.summary: Delete a custom access group (cascades members + grants)

post.summary: Add a member (user/organization/persona/app/token) to an access group

delete.summary: Remove a member from an access group

put.summary: Upsert a grant (bucket / bucket_type / row : crud) for an access group

delete.summary: Remove a grant from an access group

get.summary: Get a conversation with its messages

patch.summary: Rename a conversation

post.summary: Send a message and stream the assistant turn (SSE)
post.description: Sends a user message and streams the assistant response as Server-Sent Events (`text/event-stream`). The stream emits an `open` event, then `token`, `tool_call_started`, `tool_call_completed`, and a terminal `done` event; failures surface as an `error` event. The response is always 200 even when the conversation is missing or the turn fails — those conditions are reported in-band via the `error` event rather than an HTTP status.

patch.summary: Edit a user message

get.summary: The caller's communication/presence dial (saved row, or the open defaults)

put.summary: Replace the caller's communication/presence dial (upsert; full representation)
put.description: Masks must combine only the known AUDIENCE bits (PUBLIC=1, HUB=2, TEAM=4, ORG=8, AI=16, FRIEND=32); 0 means nobody. Blocks always override the dial, and appear_offline hides presence from everyone.

dmAudienceMask.description: AUDIENCE bitmask of who may DM the user (0 = nobody; default PUBLIC|HUB = 3)

presenceAudienceMask.description: AUDIENCE bitmask of who may see the user presence (0 = nobody; default PUBLIC|HUB = 3)

appearOffline.description: When true, presence is hidden from EVERYONE regardless of the mask

get.summary: The caller's appearance preferences (empty object when never saved)
get.description: Every adh site reads this on sign-in so a user's colour mode and accessibility settings follow them across the family (the sites span many registrable domains, so a browser-local copy cannot). A signed-out visitor follows their OS setting instead.

put.summary: Replace the caller's appearance preferences (upsert; full representation)

colorMode.description: The user's colour scheme; 'auto' follows the operating system

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

get.summary: List/search the caller's markdown documents (metadata only)
get.description: Lists the caller’s documents, most-recently-updated first. Optional filters narrow the set: `q` (free-text, case-insensitive substring across title, body, category, and tags), `category` (exact match), and `tag` (set membership).

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text query matched across title, body, category, and tags.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Restrict to documents with this exact category.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Restrict to documents whose tag set contains this tag.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Restrict to documents whose frontmatter adh_source equals this value. Used by researchUpsert to look up an existing doc without scanning the full export.

200.description: A page of document metadata, most-recently-updated first

post.summary: Create a markdown document (writes the head + version 1)

title.description: Optional; derived from frontmatter/H1 if omitted.

author.description: Author of this revision; omit to attribute to the calling customer. customer/user are pinned to the caller; other types are caller-asserted (unverified).

id.description: Id within that type's namespace (ignored for customer/user — pinned to the caller).

name.description: Display label; ignored for customer/user (taken from the principal).

get.summary: List the caller's existing category names (autocomplete source)
get.description: The account's full set of category names (content.categories), scoped to the caller and ecosystem, distinct and alphabetical — the autocomplete/browse source for the research classification UI.

get.summary: List the caller's existing tag labels (autocomplete source)
get.description: The account's full set of tag labels (content.keywords), scoped to the caller and ecosystem, distinct and alphabetical — the autocomplete/browse source for the research tag field.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

get.summary: Bulk export the caller's documents WITH content (download a whole set)

200.description: All documents (with content), ordered by createdAt

truncated.description: True if more documents remain (the export stopped at its approximate byte budget).

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

put.summary: Update a document; any real change appends a full-state version

schema.description: At least one of content/title/category/tags. A content or title change appends a full-state version (author attaches to it); a category/tags-only change updates the head in place WITHOUT a new version; a no-op returns the doc unchanged.

content.description: New raw markdown; a real change bumps current_version.

category.description: Classification label; send null to clear, omit to leave unchanged.

tags.description: Replacement tag set (trimmed + de-duplicated); omit to leave unchanged.

author.description: Author of this revision; omit to attribute to the calling customer. customer/user are pinned to the caller; other types are caller-asserted (unverified).

id.description: Id within that type's namespace (ignored for customer/user — pinned to the caller).

name.description: Display label; ignored for customer/user (taken from the principal).

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

get.summary: Get the raw markdown bytes (text/markdown), with an ETag
get.description: Returns the content verbatim. Send If-None-Match with the ETag for a 304.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

post.summary: Publish a document under an author-defined public route slug
post.description: Makes the document readable UNAUTHENTICATED at /public/users/{slug}/papers/{route}. The route is lowercase, url-safe, and UNIQUE PER AUTHOR — a collision with another of the caller’s live papers returns 409.

route.description: Public route slug: lowercase, url-safe ([a-z0-9_-], leading alphanumeric), 2–128 chars.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

post.summary: Unpublish a document (revert to a private draft, freeing its route)

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

post.summary: Finalize a document (stage='final'; content becomes immutable)
post.description: Marks the document final: content/title edits and version restores are rejected with 409 until definalize. Publication state is deliberately NOT covered — a final document can still be published/unpublished. Idempotent: re-finalizing a final document is a 200 no-op.

parameters.name: workspace
parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

post.summary: Definalize a document (revert to stage='draft'; edits work again)
post.description: Reverts a final document to an editable draft, lifting the content-immutability guard. Idempotent: definalizing a draft is a 200 no-op.

parameters.description: Scope to this WORKSPACE’s owning principal (the caller’s own customer slug, or an organization the caller belongs to). Omitted: the caller’s own documents. Unknown/foreign slug: 404.

post.summary: Mark all of the caller's notifications as read

get.summary: The caller's accepted friends (each entry names the OTHER user; paginated)

200.description: A page of the caller's friends

get.summary: Pending friend requests — ?direction=received (default, the caller's inbox) | sent

200.description: A page of pending friend requests, newest first

post.summary: Send a friend request (idempotent on the unordered pair; mutual → auto-accept)
post.description: Creates a pending friendship with the caller as requester. If an edge for the unordered pair already exists in either direction no second row is created: a pending request FROM the other user is auto-accepted (both sides asked), a pending request from the caller or an accepted friendship is returned as-is, and a declined edge flips back to pending with the caller as requester. Refused (403) while a block exists in either direction between the two users.

userId.description: The customer id of the user to befriend

post.summary: Accept a pending friend request (addressee only)
Decline a pending friend request (addressee only)

delete.summary: Remove the friendship/request between the caller and a user (either party)

200.description: A page of the caller's outbound follows, newest first
A page of the caller's inbound follows, newest first

put.summary: Follow a user (idempotent — one-way, no permission semantics)
put.description: Creates the caller→user follow edge. A follow is a subscription, not a grant: it needs no consent and carries no permission weight. Idempotent — repeating the call returns the existing edge (200) instead of creating a second row.

200.description: A page of the caller's blocks, newest first

put.summary: Block a user (idempotent; severs any friendship between the pair)
put.description: Creates the caller→user block. Blocking removes any existing friendship or pending request between the two users in the same transaction, and new friend requests are refused (403) while a block exists in either direction. Follows are untouched — they carry no permission weight. Idempotent — repeating the call returns the existing block (200) instead of creating a second row.

delete.summary: Unblock a user (does NOT restore a severed friendship)

get.summary: List the caller's bookmarks (newest first, paginated)

post.summary: Add or re-affirm a bookmark (idempotent on the caller + target)

notify.description: Also subscribe to the target’s activity notifications (default false)

delete.summary: Remove one of the caller's bookmarks

delete.summary: Un-note a doc (removes the marker only; the markdown doc survives)

expiresAt.description: Omitted/null defaults to a bounded 90-day TTL (never a non-expiring token); an explicit value is honored.

delete.summary: Soft-delete a custom role (refused while assignments still reference it)

get.summary: List assignments (workspace-wide for admins; per-item for M holders)

put.summary: Grant (or replace) a role for a subject at a scope — requires M + no-escalation

feature.description: with itemId: scope the grant to one item

delete.summary: Revoke an assignment (requires M at its scope)

post.summary: Mark an item RESTRICTED (workspace-scope grants stop applying to it)
Restore an item to inherited access (deletes its restriction row)

get.summary: A subject's effective verbs + provenance (the explainer; requires M)

post.summary: Begin an upload: reserve an attachment + presigned PUT URL

sizeBytes.description: Declared size; enforced against the storage quota at init.

contentHash.description: When it matches an existing ready object, the upload is deduplicated.

200.description: Deduplicated — an identical object already exists; no new upload

get.summary: Get one of the caller's attachments by id

delete.summary: Delete an attachment (removes the R2 object, then the row)

post.summary: Confirm an upload completed → mark the attachment ready

get.summary: Get a presigned download URL for an attachment
List every live theme (suite-wide; empty in production)
Get one live theme by key

key.description: DNS-ish slug, unique among live themes

basedOn.description: the seed theme key this derives from

get.summary: Get the caller's storage quota + current usage
Get a user's storage quota + usage (admin)

put.summary: Set a user's storage quota override (admin)

get.summary: List the caller's workspaces (individual first, then orgs, then teams)
An organization workspace's roster: the distinct customers across the org's org-owned teams
